Adopted in 2011. Subject to endorsement by CCFL, CCFA and CCMAS.

Members of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in the region of Near East are indicated on the Codex website at https://www.fao.org/fao-who-codexalimentarius.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This Standard applies to harissa (red hot pepper paste) as defined in Section 2, manufactured and intended for direct human consumption. It does not apply to other traditionally-prepared types of harissa.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to harissa (red hot pepper paste) as defined in Section 2, manufactured and intended for direct human consumption. It does not apply to other traditionally-prepared types of harissa.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to harissa (red hot pepper paste) as defined in Section 2, manufactured and intended for direct human consumption. It does not apply to other traditionally-prepared types of harissa.

reference.document_title: Regional Standard for Harissa (Red Hot Pepper Paste) (Near East)

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: “Harissa” designates the preserved pulp of fresh red hot pepper, of the Capsicum annuum variety, concentrated and with the composition provided for in Section 3 and preserved using thermal treatment only.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION
number=2
level=1
“Harissa” designates the preserved pulp of fresh red hot pepper, of the Capsicum annuum variety, concentrated and with the composition provided for in Section 3 and preserved using thermal treatment only.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=5
level=1

The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: Ingredients
sections.text:
- Fresh red hot peppers of the Capsicum annuum variety
- Fresh garlic
- Coriander
- Caraway
- Salt

sections.title: Ingredients
sections.text: The peppers used in the preparation of processed Harissa shall be sufficiently ripe, wholesome, with a deep red colour, free of spoilage, rot and impurities and free of insects.

sections.title: Sensory factors
sections.text:
- Natural homogenous red color with a minimum of color on a “hunter” scale of the value of a/b not less than 1/4 and free of black particles.
- The taste shall be typical to the product, spicy (hot), free from bitterness or burned taste or any other foreign taste.
- The smell shall be typical of the product and free of foreign smells.

sections.title: FOOD HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
sections.retrieval_text:
FOOD HYGIENE
number=6
level=1
It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

page_spans.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text: The product covered by the provisions of this Standard shall be labelled in accordance with the latest edition of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985). In addition, the following specific provisions apply: The name of the product shall be horissa.

sections.title: PACKAGING AND STORAGE
sections.text: Harissa must be packed in suitable and clean containers, tightly closed, and all materials in contact with the product must be authorized for use in food packaging.

number=9

sections.title: Fill of Container
sections.text: Minimum Fill (a) The container should be well filled with the product, which should occupy not less than 90% (minus any necessary head space according to good manufacturing practices) of the water capacity of the container. The water capacity of the container is the volume of distilled water at 20˚C, which the sealed container will hold when completely filled. (b) Flexible containers should be filled as full as commercially practicable.
sections.retrieval_text:

level=2

The water capacity of the container is the volume of distilled water at 20˚C, which the sealed container will hold when completely filled. (b) Flexible containers should be filled as full as commercially practicable.

page_spans.text: Minimum Fill (a) The container should be well filled with the product, which should occupy not less than 90% (minus any necessary head space according to good manufacturing practices) of the water capacity of the container. The water capacity of the container is the volume of distilled water at 20˚C, which the sealed container will hold when completely filled. (b) Flexible containers should be filled as full as commercially practicable.

notes: Typical product quality requirement

sentences.original: Harissa must be packed in suitable and clean containers, tightly closed, and all materials in contact with the product must be authorized for use in food packaging.

classification.pattern: Compound obligation sentence listing packaging requirements and material authorization conditions

components.packing_requirements: must be packed in suitable and clean containers, tightly closed
components.material_requirements: all materials in contact with the product must be authorized for use in food packaging

classification.justification: The sentence expresses multiple mandatory requirements on packaging and materials without close match to known templates. The structure is a compound obligation listing several conditions.
notes: Contains multiple obligations in one sentence which complicates simple template matching.

classification.pattern: Obligation with comparative degree and qualified condition

notes: Qualitative fill level and commercial practicability qualification differentiate this from strict quantitative limit templates

pages.title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR HARISSA (Red hot pepper paste) (Near East)
pages.summary: Página inicial com o título principal do documento e seu código padrão, contendo texto introdutório.
Página estruturada com cinco seções principais numeradas de 1 a 5, e subtítulos hierarquizados até o nível 4 para detalhamento dos fatores de qualidade dos ingredientes e do produto final.

Página apresenta cinco seções principais numeradas (6 a 10) com um subtítulo na seção 7. O texto está claramente associado a cada nó estrutural. Não há indicação de continuidade entre páginas nesta página.
